display_name: Lapidarium True - Fase 3: Execucao por Fatias
target_phase_label: Lapidarium Fase 3 - Execucao por Fatias True

slice_matrix.title: LLM provider port boundary
source_basis: ADR 006 LLM Adapter Pattern for Providers
slice_matrix.objective: Hide direct provider SDK access behind LLMProviderPort and keep provider selection in policy or use-case code.
acceptance_criteria: The application layer speaks to a stable LLMProviderPort instead of a concrete SDK.
Groq and Claude are treated as adapters, not as application-level dependencies.
Provider selection stays in policy or use-case code.
No domain module imports a provider SDK.
blocked_if: The slice would require runtime execution
The slice would move provider SDK ownership into the domain layer
The slice would change live active-context state

slice_matrix.title: Use-case orchestration boundary
slice_matrix.objective: Keep orchestration in the application layer and prevent transport or framework concerns from becoming use-case policy.
acceptance_criteria: Use cases coordinate domain objects and outward ports.
Transport layout, filesystem layout, and secret handling stay out of use cases.
Application flow remains deterministic and testable without network access.
The orchestration boundary remains explicit in the slice plan.
blocked_if: The slice requires transport-layer changes to decide business policy
The slice depends on a live runtime or external provider call

slice_matrix.title: Domain purity and enterprise rules
slice_matrix.objective: Keep the domain layer pure, with data shapes and rules that do not depend on provider SDKs, env vars, the filesystem, or network services.
acceptance_criteria: Domain modules remain free of provider SDK imports.
Domain modules remain free of environment and filesystem reads.
Domain modules expose pure dataclasses, enums, and pure functions only.
Domain rules stay testable independently of adapters and drivers.
blocked_if: The slice would add framework ownership to the domain layer
The slice would require product or runtime authorization

slice_matrix.title: Adapter and driver surface consolidation
source_basis: TARGET_ARCHITECTURE.md Interface Adapters and Frameworks / Drivers
ADR 006 LLM Adapter Pattern for Providers
slice_matrix.objective: Make adapter boundaries explicit so framework-specific code remains replaceable and does not absorb business policy.
acceptance_criteria: Adapters translate DTOs, errors, and provider/framework shapes without owning business rules.
Framework and driver code stays at the perimeter.
The adapter layer can be replaced without changing the domain model.
Boundary translation is centralized instead of duplicated.
blocked_if: The slice would move policy into the driver layer
The slice would require live provider or network calls

slice_matrix.title: Documentation taxonomy alignment
source_basis: ADR 007 Diataxis ADR Taxonomy for Project ARIS
slice_matrix.objective: Keep explanation, reference, how-to, and tutorial content in the right document family so the architecture model stays readable and does not drift into operations.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture explanation remains separated from operational how-to content.
Decision records stay explanation-first and are not repurposed as operational instructions.
Reference pages state facts without smuggling in procedural guidance.
The documentation surface follows the Diataxis classifier declared in the target architecture.
blocked_if: The slice would require changing runtime behavior
The slice would flatten documentation families into one operational bundle

notes: The slice matrix is anchored only to explicit layer and module boundaries named in TARGET_ARCHITECTURE.md and its two ADR support documents.
This artifact is planning-only and does not authorize runtime, product, Bedrock, secrets, or environment mutation.
